questions.question: What is media?
questions.correct_answer: Different means of communication used to share information with large numbers of people
questions.explanation: Media includes television, radio, newspapers, magazines, and the internet.

questions.question: What is mass media?
questions.correct_answer: Media that reaches a very large audience at the same time
questions.explanation: Examples include television broadcasts, newspapers, and online news platforms.

questions.question: Name two examples of print media.
questions.explanation: Print media distributes information through printed publications.

questions.question: Which media mainly uses sound to communicate?
questions.explanation: Radio broadcasts information through audio programs.

questions.question: What is the main role of media in society?
questions.correct_answer: To inform people about events, issues, and developments
questions.explanation: Media spreads information that helps people understand what is happening around them.

questions.question: What is advertising?
questions.correct_answer: Promotion of products or services through media
questions.explanation: Advertisements are designed to attract customers and increase sales.

questions.question: Why are advertisements important for media companies?
questions.correct_answer: They provide a major source of income
questions.explanation: Media organizations rely on advertisements to support their operations.

questions.question: What does the watchdog role of media mean?
questions.explanation: Media investigates corruption and ensures accountability in society.

questions.question: How can media influence public opinion?
questions.correct_answer: By selecting and presenting certain information or viewpoints
questions.explanation: The way news is reported can shape how people think about issues.

questions.question: Why is media important in a democracy?
questions.correct_answer: It informs citizens and encourages discussion about public issues
questions.explanation: Informed citizens can participate better in democratic decision-making.

notes.title: Media
notes.content: Media refers to communication channels that spread information to large audiences.

notes.title: Mass Media
notes.content: Includes television, radio, newspapers, and the internet that reach millions of people.

notes.title: Advertisements
notes.content: Promote products and services and provide revenue for media organizations.

notes.title: Media as Watchdog
notes.content: Media investigates and exposes corruption and injustice.

notes.title: Media in Democracy
notes.content: Media keeps citizens informed and encourages public debate and awareness.

facts.fact: Mass media can reach millions of people within seconds through modern technology.
Advertisements are one of the main financial supports for media companies.
The way media presents information can influence people's opinions.
Free and independent media is considered essential for a healthy democracy.
